attestation.recorded_by: claude-fable-5 (agent, on the clinician's explicit written attestation in-session)
attestation.statement: Clinician reviewed and attests the red-flag question banks and escalation tiers present for low back pain and headache: the questions and their T0/T3/T4/T5 tiers (e.g. bladder/bowel dysfunction → T5; thunderclap onset → T5; meningococcal rash → T5) are clinically appropriate for Trunk 9.0 safety-netting. Clinical sign-off only; coverage limited to the presentations present (expansion separate); regulatory sign-off not provided here.
attestation.scope: the 2 records in this file (redflags-bank, dev v0.1.0)

red_flag_questions.question: Any loss of bladder or bowel control, or numbness around the back passage/genitals?
Any new weakness in the legs that is getting worse?
Any fever, or recent serious infection or IV drug use?
Any unexplained weight loss or history of cancer?
Did the headache come on suddenly and reach its worst within seconds to minutes?
Any fever with neck stiffness or a rash that doesn't fade under pressure?
Any new weakness, vision loss, confusion or difficulty speaking?
Over 50 with new headache, scalp tenderness or jaw pain when chewing?
